raw_consumer_statement:
I am submitting this complaint regarding the handling of unauthorized transaction disputes by Citibank , N.A . related to recurring charges originating from XXXX merchant descriptors on my credit card account. 

The charges are : XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX # XXXX XXXX AZ {$380.00} on XX/XX/year> XXXX XXXX  XXXX # XXXX XXXX AZ {$190.00} on XX/XX/year> I disputed multiple transactions that I did not authorize and for which I did not knowingly enter into any recurring subscription agreement. I also took proactive steps to protect my account, including attempting to cancel services, locking or replacing my card, and attempting to prevent further recurring transactions. Despite these actions, additional charges continued to be processed. 

During the dispute process, Citibank demanded that I provide a credit slip or documentation showing the merchant agreed to issue a refund. This requirement is not applicable to my dispute. My dispute is not based on a merchant agreeing to issue a refund that failed to post. My dispute is that these transactions themselves were unauthorized and lacked valid affirmative authorization for recurring billing. 

Because the charges were not authorized, no credit slip exists. By conditioning dispute resolution on documentation that would only exist if a merchant voluntarily agreed to issue a refund, Citibank effectively shifted the burden of investigation onto me rather than evaluating whether the transactions were authorized in the first place. 

Additionally, recurring charges continued even after I took steps to secure my account, which raises concerns about Citibanks controls related to stopping recurring transactions after a card is locked, replaced, or disputed. 

I am concerned this reflects a broader pattern and practice of : Reframing unauthorized transaction disputes as merchant credit disputes Imposing documentation requirements that are not relevant to unauthorized transaction claims Failing to properly investigate authorization and recurring billing consent Allowing recurring charges to continue after consumers take security actions I made good faith efforts to resolve this matter directly. However, Citibanks response indicates a failure to properly distinguish between unauthorized transaction disputes and merchant refund disputes, which materially impacts consumers ability to obtain fair dispute investigations. 

I am submitting this complaint through the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau because I believe Citibank failed to properly follow federal consumer billing investigation requirements and improperly shifted the burden of proof to me.
